title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
text: toolkit temporal reasoning. chronos provides functions radiocarbon calibration chronological analysis. also includes tools describe analyze finite time intervals.

title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
text: can install released version chronos CRAN : development version GitHub :
code: install.packages("chronos") # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("tesselle/chronos")

title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
code: ## Load packages library(chronos) ## Data from Bosch et al. 2015 data("ksarakil")  ## Calibrate multiple dates cal <- c14_calibrate(   ages = ksarakil$date,   errors = ksarakil$error,   names = ksarakil$code,   curves = "marine13",   reservoir_offsets = 53,   reservoir_errors = 43,   from = 50000, to = 0 )  ## Plot plot(cal, panel.first = graphics::grid())

title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
text: Please note chronos project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project, agree abide terms.

title: Calendar — Calendar-class
text: unit character string giving name year unit. days numeric vector giving average length year solar days.

title: Calibrated Radiocarbon Ages — CalibratedAges-class
text: S4 class represent calibrated radiocarbon ages.

title: Calibrated Radiocarbon Ages — CalibratedAges-class
text: labels character vector specifying names ages (e.g. laboratory codes). ages numeric vector giving BP ages calibrated. errors numeric vector giving standard deviation ages calibrated. curves character vector specifying calibration curves used.

title: Calibrated Radiocarbon Ages — CalibratedAges-class
text: start length-one numeric vector specifying beginning calibrated time range. resolution length-one numeric vector specifying resolution calibrated time range. F14C logical scalar: calendar TimeScale object specfiying time scale. status integer vector specifying calibration status. must one "0" (OK), "1" (calibration range) "2" (may extend calibration range).

title: Calibrated SPD — CalibratedSPD-class
text: S4 class represent summed probability distributions (SPD) radiocarbon dates.

title: Calibrated SPD — CalibratedSPD-class
text: start length-one numeric vector specifying beginning calibrated time range. resolution length-one numeric vector specifying resolution calibrated time range. calendar TimeScale object specfiying time scale.

title: F14C — F14C
code: BP14C_to_F14C(ages, errors, ...)  F14C_to_BP14C(ratios, errors, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric BP14C_to_F14C(ages, errors)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric F14C_to_BP14C(ratios, errors)

title: F14C — F14C
text: ages numeric vector giving radiocarbon ages. errors numeric vector giving standard deviation ages/ratios. ... Currently used. ratios numeric vector giving F14C ratios.

title: F14C — F14C
text: Bronk Ramsey, C. (2008). RADIOCARBON DATING: REVOLUTIONS UNDERSTANDING. Archaeometry, 50:249-275. doi:10.1111/j.1475-4754.2008.00394.x .

title: TimeScale — TimeScale-class
text: label character string giving abbreviated label time scale. name character string giving name time scale. epoch numeric value giving epoch year years counted (Gregorian astronomical years). scale integer specifying number years represented one unit. direction integer specifying years counted backwards (\(-1\)) forwards (\(1\)) epoch.

title: 14C Calibration — c14_calibrate
code: c14_calibrate(ages, errors, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric c14_calibrate(   ages,   errors,   names = NULL,   curves = "intcal20",   reservoir_offsets = 0,   reservoir_errors = 0,   from = 55000,   to = 0,   resolution = 1,   normalize = TRUE,   F14C = FALSE,   drop = TRUE,   eps = 1e-06 )

title: 14C Calibration — c14_calibrate
text: ages numeric vector giving BP ages calibrated. errors numeric vector giving standard deviation ages calibrated. ... Currently used. names character vector specifying names ages (e.g. laboratory codes). curves character vector specifying calibration curve used. Different curves can specified per dated sample. reservoir_offsets numeric vector giving offset values marine reservoir effect (defaults 0; .e. offset). reservoir_errors numeric vector giving offset value errors marine reservoir effect (defaults 0; .e. offset). length-one numeric vector specifying earliest data calibrate , cal. BP years. length-one numeric vector specifying latest data calibrate , cal. BP years. resolution length-one numeric vector specifying temporal resolution (years) calibration. normalize logical scalar: calibration normalized? F14C logical scalar: calibration carried F14C space? drop logical scalar: years zero probability discarded? TRUE (default), results narrower time range. eps length-one numeric value giving cutoff calibration values removed.

title: 14C Calibration — c14_calibrate
text: Adapted rcarbon::calibrate() Andrew Bevan Enrico Crema.

title: 14C Calibration — c14_calibrate
text: Bronk Ramsey, C. (2008). RADIOCARBON DATING: REVOLUTIONS UNDERSTANDING. Archaeometry, 50:249-275. doi:10.1111/j.1475-4754.2008.00394.x .

title: 14C Calibration — c14_calibrate
code: ## Calibrate a single date cal <- c14_calibrate(300, 20) plot(cal)   ## Calibrate multiple dates cal <- c14_calibrate(   ages = c(5000, 4500),   errors = c(45, 35),   names = c("X", "Y") ) plot(cal, panel.first = graphics::grid())   plot(cal, density = TRUE, interval = FALSE)  plot(cal, density = FALSE, interval = TRUE)   ## Convert BP scale to CE CE <- project(cal, "CE") plot(CE)   ## Convert BP scale to b2k b2k <- project(cal, "b2k") plot(b2k)

title: 14C Calibration — c14_combine
code: c14_combine(ages, errors, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric c14_combine(ages, errors, groups = NULL)

title: 14C Calibration — c14_combine
text: ages numeric vector giving BP ages calibrated. errors numeric vector giving standard deviation ages calibrated. ... Currently used. groups factor sense .factor(groups) defines groups combine . NULL (default), dates combined. NAs treated isolated dates.

title: 14C Calibration — c14_combine
text: Ward, G. K. Wilson, S. R. (1978). Procedures Comparing Combining Radiocarbon Age Determinations: Critique. Archaeometry 20(1): 19‑31. doi:10.1111/j.1475-4754.1978.tb00208.x .

title: 14C Calibration Curve — c14_curve
code: c14_curve(x, ...)  # S4 method for character c14_curve(x)

title: 14C Calibration Curve — c14_curve
text: x character string naming calibration curve (see details). ... Currently used.

title: 14C Calibration Curve — c14_curve
text: three-column data.frame: calibrated age BP, uncalibrated age BP standard deviation.

title: Plot Calibrated Radiocarbon Ages — c14_plot
code: # S4 method for CalibratedAges,missing plot(   x,   density = TRUE,   interval = TRUE,   level = 0.954,   warnings = TRUE,   decreasing = TRUE,   xlim = NULL,   main = NULL,   sub = NULL,   ann = graphics::par("ann"),   axes = TRUE,   frame.plot = FALSE,   panel.first = NULL,   panel.last = NULL,   ... )  # S4 method for CalibratedSPD,missing plot(   x,   xlim = NULL,   ylim = NULL,   main = NULL,   sub = NULL,   ann = graphics::par("ann"),   axes = TRUE,   frame.plot = FALSE,   panel.first = NULL,   panel.last = NULL,   ... )

title: Plot Calibrated Radiocarbon Ages — c14_plot
text: x CalibratedAges CalibratedSPD object. density logical scalar: density drawn? interval logical scalar: highest posterior density interval drawn? level length-one numeric vector giving confidence level. warnings logical scalar: warnings plotted? decreasing logical scalar: sort order decreasing? xlim length-two numeric vector giving x limits plot. main character string giving main title plot. sub character string giving subtitle plot. ann logical scalar: default annotation (title x, y z axis labels) appear plot? axes logical scalar: axes drawn plot? frame.plot logical scalar: box drawn around plot? panel.first expression evaluated plot axes set plotting takes place. can useful drawing background grids. panel.last expression evaluated plotting taken place axes, title box added. ... graphical parameters may also passed arguments function. ylim length-two numeric vector giving y limits plot.

title: Plot Calibrated Radiocarbon Ages — c14_plot
text: plot() called side-effects: results graphic displayed. Invisibly returns x.

title: Plot Calibrated Radiocarbon Ages — c14_plot
code: ## Calibrate a single date cal <- c14_calibrate(300, 20) plot(cal)   ## Calibrate multiple dates cal <- c14_calibrate(   ages = c(5000, 4500),   errors = c(45, 35),   names = c("X", "Y") ) plot(cal, panel.first = graphics::grid())   plot(cal, density = TRUE, interval = FALSE)  plot(cal, density = FALSE, interval = TRUE)   ## Convert BP scale to CE CE <- project(cal, "CE") plot(CE)   ## Convert BP scale to b2k b2k <- project(cal, "b2k") plot(b2k)

title: Calendar Converter — convert
code: convert(from, to, ...)  # S4 method for character,character convert(from, to)  # S4 method for TimeScale,TimeScale convert(from, to)

title: Calendar Converter — convert
text: TimeScale object describing source era. TimeScale object describing target era. ... Currently used.

title: Calendar Converter — convert
text: function called single numeric argument (years) converts years one calendar another.

title: Calendar Converter — convert
code: ## Define time scales BP <- era("BP") AD <- era("AD")  ## Make conversion functions BP_to_AD <- convert(BP, AD) AD_to_BP <- convert(AD, BP)  ## Convert years BP_to_AD(0) #> [1] 1950 AD_to_BP(1950) #> [1] 0

title: Time Scales — era
code: era(object, ...)  # S4 method for character era(object)  # S4 method for CalibratedAges era(object)  # S4 method for CalibratedSPD era(object)

title: Time Scales — era
text: object character string specifying time scale (see details). ... Currently used.

title: Time Scales — era
text: following time scales available: BP Present. BC Christ. BCE Common Era. AD Anno Domini. CE Common Era. b2k Years 2000.

title: Time Scales — era
code: ## Define time scales BP <- era("BP") AD <- era("AD")  ## Make conversion functions BP_to_AD <- convert(BP, AD) AD_to_BP <- convert(AD, BP)  ## Convert years BP_to_AD(0) #> [1] 1950 AD_to_BP(1950) #> [1] 0

title: HPD Regions — hpdi
code: hpdi(object, density, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric hpdi(object, density, level = 0.954)  # S4 method for CalibratedAges,missing hpdi(object, level = 0.954)

title: HPD Regions — hpdi
text: object numeric vector giving coordinates points density estimated CalibratedAges object. density numeric vector giving estimated density values. density missing object ǹumeric vector, density estimates computed object. ... Currently used. level length-one numeric vector giving confidence level.

title: HPD Regions — hpdi
text: list numeric  matrix giving lower upper boundaries HPD interval associated probabilities.

title: HPD Regions — hpdi
text: Hyndman, R. J. (1996). Computing graphing highest density regions. American Statistician, 50: 120-126. doi:10.2307/2684423 .

text: Bosch, M. D., Mannino, M. ., Prendergast, . L., O'Connell, T. C., Demarchi, B., Taylor, S. M., Niven, L., van der Plicht, J. Hublin, J.-J. (2015). New Chronology Ksâr 'Akil (Lebanon) Supports Levantine Route Modern Human Dispersal Europe. Proceedings National Academy Sciences 112(25): 7683-8. doi:10.1073/pnas.1501529112 .

title: Summed Probability Distributions — spd
text: Computes summed probability distributions (SPD) radiocarbon dates.

title: Summed Probability Distributions — spd
code: spd(object, ...)  # S4 method for CalibratedAges spd(object, normalize_date = FALSE, normalize_spd = FALSE)

title: Summed Probability Distributions — spd
text: object CalibratedAges object. ... Currently used. normalize_date logical scalar: total probability mass calibrated dates normalised (sum unity within time-span analysis)? normalize_spd logical scalar: total probability mass SPD normalised (sum unity)?

title: Summed Probability Distributions — spd
code: ## Radiocarbon data from Bosch et al. 2015 data("ksarakil")  ## Calibrate cal <- c14_calibrate(   ages = ksarakil$date,   errors = ksarakil$error,   names = ksarakil$code,   curves = "marine13",   reservoir_offsets = 53,   reservoir_errors = 43,   from = 50000, to = 0 ) plot(cal)   ## SPD s <- spd(cal) plot(s)
